📘

Statistical Simulation — (Full Solutions)

Q1. What is simulation? How does statistical simulation differ from deterministic simulation?
Q2. What are the steps of conducting a simulation model?

Q6. Use control variates to estimate θ=∫01e−x1+x2 dx\theta=\int_0^1 \frac{e^{-x}}{1+x^2}\,dxθ=∫01​1+x2e−x​dx, where θ=E[g(X)]\theta=E[g(X)]θ=E[g(X)] for X∼U(0,1)X\sim U(0,1)X∼U(0,1) and g(x)=e−x1+x2g(x)=\frac{e^{-x}}{1+x^2}g(x)=1+x2e−x​. (a) Use f1(x)=11+x2f_1(x)=\frac{1}{1+x^2}f1​(x)=1+x21​; (b) use f2(x)=x1+x2f_2(x)=\frac{x}{1+x^2}f2​(x)=1+x2x​; (c) compare both with simple Monte Carlo — which gives greater variance reduction?
Q7. Explain control variate and regression for MC integration. Approximate the integral of Q5, I=∫0111+x dxI=\int_0^1 \frac{1}{1+x}\,dxI=∫01​1+x1​dx, using the control-variate and regression MC method.
Group 2 — Variance Reduction & Random Number Generation (Q8–Q14)
Q8. What do you mean by antithetic variates? What are the advantages of antithetic variates in MC integration? Discuss how these variates can be used as control variates.
Q9. How do you use stratified sampling for MC simulations? Using stratified sampling, approximate θ=∫1411+x dx\theta=\int_1^4 \frac{1}{1+\sqrt{x}}\,dxθ=∫14​1+x​1​dx. Compare with simple MC and the exact value.
Q10. How would you generate a random permutation? Write down the steps.
Q11. How would you generate pseudo-random numbers using the linear congruential method? Explain with an example.
Q12. What are the additive congruential and multiplicative congruential methods? Explain them with examples.
Q13. Using the linear congruential method, generate the first 3 random numbers with seed 298529852985 and parameters a=32, c=3, m=5821a=32,\ c=3,\ m=5821a=32, c=3, m=5821. Report the random numbers to 5 decimal places.
Q14. Using the multiplicative congruence method, generate the first 10 random numbers with seed 693153693153693153 and parameters a=73a=7^3a=73 and m=217−1m=2^{17}-1m=217−1.

Q15. Let f(x)=41+x2f(x)=\frac{4}{1+x^2}f(x)=1+x24​. Use simple Monte Carlo integration to compute I(f)=∫0141+x2 dxI(f)=\int_0^1 \frac{4}{1+x^2}\,dxI(f)=∫01​1+x24​dx.
Q16. Write a program that implements the “hit-or-miss” Monte Carlo integration algorithm. Find the estimate of I=∫014(1−x2) dxI=\int_0^1 4(1-x^2)\,dxI=∫01​4(1−x2)dx.
Q17. Approximate I=∫01cos⁡x esin⁡x dxI=\int_0^1 \cos x\,e^{\sin x}\,dxI=∫01​cosxesinxdx by MC sampling with n=10,100,500,103,104,105,106,107n=10,100,500,10^3,10^4,10^5,10^6,10^7n=10,100,500,103,104,105,106,107. For each experiment, compute the sample mean, standard error, and relative error.
Q18. Use a stratified random sample to integrate I=∫01eu−1e−1 duI=\int_0^1 \frac{e^{u}-1}{e-1}\,duI=∫01​e−1eu−1​du. What do you recommend for the number of intervals (2 or 3) and sample sizes? What is the efficiency gain over simple Monte Carlo?
Q19. Explain the test of randomness with an example. Approximate the test of randomness for large samples.
Group 4 — Inverse Transform & Discrete Generation (Q20–Q24)
Q20. Discuss the inverse transformation method to simulate data from a continuous distribution. How would you generate a random sample from an exponential distribution with mean λ\lambdaλ?
Q21. Discuss the inverse transformation method for a discrete distribution. Write the algorithm to draw a sample from Binomial(5,0.4)(5,0.4)(5,0.4), and the R code to simulate 1000 values (also via the Bernoulli distribution).
Q22. Write down the algorithm to generate an (n,p)(n,p)(n,p) (Binomial) random variate.
Q23. Write down the algorithm to simulate a random variable from the Poisson distribution with parameter mmm.
Q24. Let XXX be a geometric variable with parameter ppp. Show that X=⌊ln⁡Uln⁡(1−p)⌋X=\left\lfloor \frac{\ln U}{\ln(1-p)} \right\rfloorX=⌊ln(1−p)lnU​⌋, where U∼Uniform(0,1)U\sim\text{Uniform}(0,1)U∼Uniform(0,1). Write the algorithm to generate XXX.
